| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Take your credit card. name: Jon Ossoff
subject: I need you by my side
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I’m the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won in 2024. Several ultra-MAGA opponents have entered the race. And GOP-aligned Super PACs are already spending millions attacking me.
Peter, we are just hours away from my mid-month fundraising deadline.
I’m the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won in 2024. Several ultra-MAGA opponents have entered the race. And GOP-aligned Super PACs are already spending millions attacking me.
This is the fight ahead. And I need you by my side.
Before you turn in: **[Can you split a $5 donation between my campaign and Heartland Patriots before midnight to help me win in Georgia?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/101661?refcode=em20260315-10170&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10170%2E614464%2EKFujPT)**
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/101661?refcode=em20260315-10170&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=10170%2E614464%2EKFujPT)**
When I first ran for Senate five years ago, it was because I believed the election of someone so deeply unfit for the presidency posed a danger to the country.
Many people said it couldn’t be done. But you believed — not just in me, but that our Republic is worth defending, and Georgia could lead the way.
Then we won. Not just a Senate seat, but the Senate majority for Democrats.
Now Trump has launched a power grab unprecedented in our history, abusing his power and trying to poison our Republic with fear and intimidation.
Americans are now living in fear of their own government.
I’m fighting back, but I can’t do this alone.
I need you with me.
**[Please, if you can make it work, split what you can between me and Heartland Patriots before our midnight fundraising deadline.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/101661?refcode=em20260315-10170&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=10170%2E614464%2EKFujPT)**
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/101661?refcode=em20260315-10170&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=10170%2E614464%2EKFujPT)**
As always,
Jon Ossoff

| [](https://www.facebook.com/lucaskuncemo/) | [](https://twitter.com/lucaskuncemo) | [](https://www.instagram.com/lucaskuncemo/)
---|---|---

name: Sarah McBride
subject: We’re so close
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
Our $15,000 mid-month goal is tonight at midnight.
# [](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1269?t=1&akid=2950%2E21716%2EiPBdq4)
I’ll keep this message short because we are _fast_ _approaching_ our mid-month deadline.
We’re closing in on our $15,000 goal, but we’re not quite there yet. With just hours to go, we can’t afford to fall short.
**Here’s why this matters:**
→ Benchmarks like these show both our supporters and opponents that we have what it takes to defend this seat and support Democrats across the country as we fight to take back the House in November.
→ Missing our goal would put us behind at this pivotal moment in a midterm election year.
→ I rely on you — grassroots supporters who give $5, $10, $20 at a time — because I believe in a government that works for the people, not just the billionaires.
**[So, can I count on you to give whatever you can afford to help us reach our $15,000 goal before midnight?](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260315-1.gk6gNz&refcode=em20260315-2950&amount=5&t=2&akid=2950%2E21716%2EiPBdq4)**

This team has come through before, and I know we can do it again.
Whether you’re able to donate today or not, I am grateful to have your support.
Thank you,
Sarah McBride

name: Emily Randall
subject: I’m running
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
I’ve driven, hiked, biked, and ferried across the 6th District this past year. I’ve spoken to so many of you who are struggling with the high costs of healthcare, housing, and groceries.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/127380714/607869305/1129794561?amount=10&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2026.03.15_Reelection&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYzUzM2ExYS04ZjIwLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kTOGBTyHaQHWXPsbG2dkH3flIabeKbbUkZScmJQRcd4=) Peter, I wanted to let you know: **I’m running for reelection!** I joined Congress at an unusual time in our history. (That’s an understatement.) There is no precedent for the unique combination of challenges we face today, from ICE to the climate crisis to the fascist regime in the White House. But every day since I was sworn in, I’ve fought to bring your voices, the voices of everyday working people, into the halls of power in Washington, D.C.

I’ve driven, hiked, biked, and ferried across the 6th District this past year. I’ve spoken to so many of you who are struggling with the high costs of healthcare, housing, and groceries. You’re sick and tired of Big Money in politics – and furious at politicians who refuse to act. Your stories are what drive me to do whatever I can to MAGA madness, stop the billionaires-first agenda, and deliver results to make life better for my neighbors. That’s why we’ve thrown out the old playbook and adopted an inside-outside strategy, where we do everything possible to stop the damage in Congress while organizing like hell to take back the House in November. It’s an unusual strategy for a freshman in Congress, but as I said, these are unusual times. **To fuel our 2026 political program, we’re calling on our powerhouse team of grassroots donors – over 11,000 strong! – to chip in any amount to win here in Washington and in swing districts around the country.** [_**Our launch goal is $36,000 (6th District x 6th District), and it'll go a long way toward winning here in Washington and flipping the House in clean_body: I’ve driven, hiked, biked, and ferried across the 6th District this past year. I’ve spoken to so many of you who are struggling with the high costs of healthcare, housing, and groceries. | = Peter, I wanted to let you know: I’m running for reelection! I joined Congress at an unusual time in our history. (That’s an understatement.) There is no precedent for the unique combination of challenges we face today, from ICE to the climate crisis to the fascist regime in the White House. But every day since I was sworn in, I’ve fought to bring your voices, the voices of everyday working people, into the halls of power in Washington, D.C. I’ve driven, hiked, biked, and ferried across the 6th District this past year. I’ve spoken to so many of you who are struggling with the high costs of healthcare, housing, and groceries. You’re sick and tired of Big Money in politics – and furious at politicians who refuse to act. Your stories are what drive me to do whatever I can to MAGA madness, stop the billionaires-first agenda, and deliver results to make life better for my neighbors. That’s why we’ve thrown out the old playbook and adopted an inside-outside strategy, where we do everything possible to stop the damage in Congress while organizing like hell to take back the House in November. It’s an unusual strategy for a freshman in Congress, but as I said, these are unusual times. To fuel our 2026 political program, we’re calling on our powerhouse team of grassroots donors – over 11,000 strong! – to chip in any amount to win here in Washington and in swing districts around the country. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = It’s the honor of my life to serve Washington’s 6th Congressional District and represent the community that raised me. Thank you so much 💙 – Emily | | Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. Yesterday in Rome, GA, was special. Spending time with Secretary Pete Buttigieg and meeting so many people from across Northwest Georgia reminds me why this campaign matters. Our communities deserve leadership that shows up and works for them. I'm running against Donald Trump's handpicked candidate in a runoff election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene because Leadership Matters. | [](https://substack.com/redirect/fa81fd0b-b235-4402-93d3-e7dc88630d00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I'm reminded of the song "In The Eye Of The Storm" by Ryan Stevenson as I pick up a yard full of branches and palm trees that were strung all over as Hurricane Helene passed through parts of Florida.
We hunkered down praying for mercy. Losing power and getting strong winds was nothing compared to other parts of the south east. North Carolina, South Carolina and parts of TN have been devastated. Beautiful small towns destroyed by water. People left homeless. No power, water, food and even some loosing their lives. A horrific turn of events.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9oZWFydGxhbmRqb3VybmFsLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.1zBAlUMGgLy_S0zjgq69wGRlkdbQ1hUsRdNtJh7MCRE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=191038535)
I just wonder... shouldn't our government care a bit more and give a bit more assistance? They are estimating in the billions to rebuild. Now parts of FEMA are somehow out of money they say? Maybe our bureaucrats in DC shouldn't have given so much of our tax payer money to the Ukrainian government and kept some for the hard working Americans. Talk about taking care of Our Own right now!
We should be taking care of our hurting people instead of abroad?
Trillions in debt and now this? I feel like all Americans are sighing and waiting to exhale until another 30 days for the next election.
America can't stand another weak administration. We need strong leadership in these trying times. Someone we can turn to and they can take the helm and say "With God all things will be alright.
It is a definite struggle to find joy right now. The stresses of this world are looming; Expensive groceries, gas, open boarders and at the brink of WW3.
So MUCH Too MUCH
This is when I have to hold on to Gods promises knowing He is in control. When I'm scared I can turn to Him. He can shelter us in the storm.
Instead of being scared I take a breath and sing loud for all to hear.
In the eye of the storm you remain in control, in the middle of the war you guard my soul. You alone are the anchor when my sails are torn. Your love surrounds me, in the eye of the storm.
Dear Lord, have mercy on us. Please be with the hurting people who need you and have been affected by the storm. Help them to know you are a strong tower to where they can run. Amen!

Oat Meal Cookie

**INGREDIENTS**
* 2 sticks of salted butter
* 1/2 cup of crisco
* 2 tsp vanilla
* 1/2 tsp sea salt
* 1 tsp cinnamon
* 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
* 1 cup sugar
* 1 1/2 tsp baking soda
* 3 cups oats
* 2 eggs
* 3 cups flour

**DIRECTIONS**

Preheat oven to 375*

In a bowl blend butter and crisco then add eggs and vanilla and blend
Then add all other ingredients and mix well.

You can add:
1/2 raisins, 1 cup chocolate chips, 1/2cup pecans, 1/2 cups white chocolate chips
Toffee bits, dried cranberries .Anything you prefer. Add in and mix well
Then take a large spoonful and place on cookie sheet
Bake for 11-13 mins let cool and enjoy

_Editorial comments expressed in this column are the sole opinion of the writer._

name: David Hogg
subject: Barbara Jordan
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Women deserve real power in the rooms where decisions are made.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260315-1&t=1&akid=3250%2E587540%2EJOb8SD)
Peter,
March is **Women’s History Month** — a moment to celebrate the women who’ve shaped our history, strengthened our communities, and pushed our country forward.
One of those women is **Barbara Jordan** , an incredible leader who left a lasting legacy that’s still alive today.
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260315_1_womens.oznd5-&refcode=em20260315-3250&t=2&akid=3250%2E587540%2EJOb8SD)
Barbara Jordan was first elected to the Texas Senate in 1966 at just 30 years old. She was the first Black woman elected to that body.
Then, she was elected to Congress when she was 36 — and she made history again, reshaping what leadership in America looked like.
Jordan fought for civil rights, voting rights, and immigration reform. She developed Texas’ first minimum wage law and played an important role in investigating the Watergate scandal and the impeachment of Richard Nixon.
When women are in office, they bring lived experiences and perspectives that too often get ignored in politics.
And that’s exactly why Leaders We Deserve is working to elect more women to public office — especially young women who are ready to step up and lead.
We’ve already elected inspiring women like Molly Cook, who is fighting for her Senate district in Texas every day.
This year, we’re supporting candidates like Lauren Babb Tomlinson and Nabeela Syed who are ready to fight for their own communities.
**These leaders are following in Barbara Jordan’s footsteps.** They aren’t waiting their turn — they are stepping up _now_ because this moment demands it.
But we know that running for office isn’t easy. It’s not cheap, either. And money is one of the biggest obstacles facing young progressive candidates today.
That’s why Leaders We Deserve exists, to make it possible for young candidates to compete. Without us, they will be buried by a flood of corporate cash and establishment attacks. When we level the playing field, our candidates can win.
**[This Women’s History Month, I am asking you to pitch in $5 now to help us keep breaking down barriers for young candidates, including women like Molly, Lauren, and Nabeela who are the epitome of the leaders we deserve.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260315_1_womens.oznd5-&refcode=em20260315-3250&amount=5&t=3&akid=3250%2E587540%2EJOb8SD)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260315_1_womens.oznd5-&amount=5&refcode=em20260315-3250&t=22&akid=3250%2E587540%2EJOb8SD)**
The next Barbara Jordan is already out there. Our job is to help her get elected.
Thank you for helping us support great women leaders. And thank you to all the women leaders who paved the way for our candidates.
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260315-1&t=23&refcode2=3250_587540_JOb8SD&akid=3250%2E587540%2EJOb8SD)**

**National Republicans are already pouring MILLIONS of dollars into this race — and experts say this race could cost $100 million in total.**
With polls this tight, getting drowned out by MAGA spending would spell out disaster for Democrats’ chances of flipping the Senate majority this year. So we need to respond fast, null.

Janet Mills

Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. | Friend,
Across the nation, the electoral collapse of the MAGA movement is accelerating.
Just last night Dems in GA pulled off a win in MTG's old House district, the 2nd reddest district in the entire state, and in NH a Dem pulled off a shocking win by flipping a deep red State House district.
In Wisconsin, another shocking win is on the way. Dems can permanently capture this key swing state's Supreme Court, and expand the current 4-3 blue majority to 5-2, on April 7th 2026. And now another GOP Justice just announced their retirement meaning in 2027 the Dem majority could grow to 6-1:

| Why this matters: if Democrats get an unbreakable 6-1 majority on the WI Court this means MAGA will never pass an abortion ban here, they can never gerrymander maps, and if God forbid a Republican is ever elected Gov. again, the court can cripple his or her agenda ensuring they accomplish nothing.
Look at a state like Maryland, Democrats have controlled it for over 100 years. This is because we've always held that state's high court and the GOP was never able to gerrymander or enact voter suppression.
Winning this race and then stealing another conservative court seat in 2027 is the foundation of a dark blue turn for this swing state that will ensure it never goes red again.
100 years of Democratic control in Wisconsin starts here. [We have less than one month to pull this off. Peter, This is a five-alarm emergency that requires your immediate attention.
---
**With a full-scale war now underway in Iran, we have received dozens of urgent requests from countries in the region to help evacuate U.S. citizens.**
---
Airspace is rapidly being closed down and many of your fellow Americans require safe passage. **Fortunately, Heroes for Humanity was built for moments like this.** We have been preparing for weeks and are asking you right now for your urgent help to fund rapid evacuation capability, medical response, logistics, open source intelligence (OSINT), assets on the ground, and strategic planning, support, and assistance in order to provide life-saving humanitarian relief.

[ DONATE NOW ](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=2bd452ccaaff4696b9388aebd38f8765&_e=ZAn3T5oHg2969ZScevqYTU6mG7jFIEQPKb55nu8-B37qMPqAc21vJN-odGiiMFSWzKggeRkdRrBAEuBtznUBLjUAR5Lr_eoXtaPL9GfMHamXxzJ7LXYrbBMEE8VgX3x7dzoSimcokbLENnAmxOFP4uKHHaVH5wOiBZuk2Je-SuIa7PwOWASpHTcYk1Hf-6piXZXFaS_XJ2AGjBLXUABh5_KAd4aBeYXL-1zNlSYtcgX5XobSGOxMne1EDvgfsoUN8MvusXXznc02Vl9PJi6vri2M8iyouqtXdxBjx65a6hE-LW-tjcbywSZ0UvoHR-0TyZSXG70ixmp6tiiJd3_szjS3hb4vxwcr3ze01LOwIuxzNAWfEWvfh9286mOd67Wia2_edRIYhJ-GEigpX2xIzrlMVfwCXvpOv83sb7F3fEN_pS58CApOgZ1jjobAPBfByvr4mREKyWbn3EPoijVocrLW8X_r3NhjTkl3IRv_K4Asaz2hJ0PWWfhnCs9O9ljBV-xXPdwYIL41p8pyJXJJGAs8qog3E99RCn1RiPSEra9CPSqtdWh7wWSdKIoAqK-k_6fPB2WppzCjdODoQnOZkaoXx81a8irDzh5MA8q7qJJgD5nCKDpxR7AmOUXrMs1asTrfQGj_N1TFJDddt6CQkAAqJqq0e0fhjIT57whbQChkQahxxlXYuBTy9WMw7mfJjPXbTAvsJXABLI0RlIn2QQ%3D%3D)

This is Dennis Price. I'm a Marine Special operator and private military contractor with over 15 deployments who now leads rescue operations, humanitarian and medical aid – all led by the Holy Spirit “to free the oppressed, so others may live.” **Since 2022, I've been the founder and leader of Heroes for Humanity – a network of special operators, veterans, first responders, medical professionals, and everyday people committed to being the hands and feet of Jesus.** We were born in the chaos of Afghanistan's fall in 2021 – where I personally helped evacuate 77 Americans and vetted Afghan allies, even swimming the Panjshir River behind enemy lines to pull people out. And just the other day, we rescued 8 families from the chaos and violence in Mexico. But our story goes much deeper than even that.
---
---
In 2022, we **deployed to Ukraine for nine months** during the invasion, **delivered medical aid to orphanages** , **supported 65 wounded veterans** , and **ran precision rescues and logistics.** And between 2023 and 2025, we **rescued trafficked children stateside** (including a 12-year-old trapped 45 days in California and a 16-year-old in Virginia), **aided persecuted Christians in China** , **responded to the Texas floods and hurricanes in North Carolina and Florida** , **evacuated 92 American families from Israel** during the 12-Day War, and provided aid after earthquakes and crises in Thailand, Burma, Cuba, Zambia, and more. As you can see, we've been busy all across the globe, _**but right now our only focus is getting Americans out of harm's way in the Middle East where things can go very wrong very quickly.**_ [**Our urgent goal is $1,000,000 and your immediate support enables rescue, medical aid, and protection for the vulnerable. Will you rush in an emergency, time-sensitive donation right now?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=2bd452ccaaff4696b9388aebd38f8765&_e=ZAn3T5oHg2969ZScevqYTU6mG7jFIEQPKb55nu8-B37qMPqAc21vJN-odGiiMFSWzKggeRkdRrBAEuBtznUBLjUAR5Lr_eoXtaPL9GfMHamXxzJ7LXYrbBMEE8VgX3x7dzoSimcokbLENnAmxOFP4uKHHaVH5wOiBZuk2Je-SuIa7PwOWASpHTcYk1Hf-6piXZXFaS_XJ2AGjBLXUABh5_KAd4aBeYXL-1zNlSYtcgX5XobSGOxMne1EDvgfsoUN3Bdqi4i8q0CqC9yn03PLgrA-LqF_rOVtpkQXxmSgtXXhW5MK19XFKw0SE6Op8acXtUl87yZXd8WXa3-Ra0QJx6CnuPEWbxJZhih5KCELcVPjUFq9s_aRrFhLk7UahU7uapRV2QWZr19vl-dzBJDYGnGJVnu8cgDk1SasKkPn052v4tQ9LTen0EVyrLwPXCzCoXIIu9DTNLhczeL5_F5bV7jddZrcsK5B_5jxyXNG60Cxk4iM9T6SaF0urqaigKdUtlF9gLuZaPeEzlUCwKejte7sb_89rn3vpVslOFxfx9Avxt2ZICR8iVxFs5pSmA7ocq9jU12oneewNKETruAeWpwwJh5EpoHu_hWgiunxiOydTlHzIobcO2nFVsgLVki38TFDOF-UJIMehIfKPrO0a0FcrGfkTNkXqqyf9VL5cq8OMb3vf-bUs285HJd_8hBesI5hDJE6OdX8xu9ZkT9ccw%3D%3D) **Lives hang in the balance and you can make the difference.**

clean_body: We have received dozens of urgent requests This is a five-alarm emergency that requires your immediate attention. | | | Peter, This is a five-alarm emergency that requires your immediate attention. With a full-scale war now underway in Iran, we have received dozens of urgent requests from countries in the region to help evacuate U.S. citizens. Airspace is rapidly being closed down and many of your fellow Americans require safe passage. Fortunately, Heroes for Humanity was built for moments like this. We have been preparing for weeks and are asking you right now for your urgent help to fund rapid evacuation capability, medical response, logistics, open source intelligence (OSINT), assets on the ground, and strategic planning, support, and assistance in order to provide life-saving humanitarian relief. This is Dennis Price. I'm a Marine Special operator and private military contractor with over 15 deployments who now leads rescue operations, humanitarian and medical aid – all led by the Holy Spirit “to free the oppressed, so others may live.” Since 2022, I've been the founder and leader of Heroes for Humanity – a network of special operators, veterans, first responders, medical professionals, and everyday people committed to being the hands and feet of Jesus. We were born in the chaos of Afghanistan's fall in 2021 – where I personally helped evacuate 77 Americans and vetted Afghan allies, even swimming the Panjshir River behind enemy lines to pull people out. And just the other day, we rescued 8 families from the chaos and violence in Mexico. But our story goes much deeper than even that. In 2022, we deployed to Ukraine for nine months during the invasion, delivered medical aid to orphanages , supported 65 wounded veterans , and ran precision rescues and logistics. And between 2023 and 2025, we rescued trafficked children stateside (including a 12-year-old trapped 45 days in California and a 16-year-old in Virginia), aided persecuted Christians in China , responded to the Texas floods and hurricanes in North Carolina and Florida , evacuated 92 American families from Israel during the 12-Day War, and provided aid after earthquakes and crises in Thailand, Burma, Cuba, Zambia, and more. As you can see, we've been busy all across the globe, but right now our only focus is getting Americans out of harm's way in the Middle East where things can go very wrong very quickly. Lives hang in the balance and you can make the difference. Beyond your support, we ask for prayer, wisdom, protection for the innocent, and strength for those making difficult decisions. Our mission remains clear:protect the vulnerable, serve with integrity, and act with faith. Thank you for your time, and further updates will be provided as the security environment allows. Respectfully submitted, Dennis Price CEO & Founder Heroes for Humanity “In order for evil to prevail, good men must stand by and do nothing.” | SERVING THE VULNERABLE. SHARING THE GOSPEL CHANGING LIVES. Heroes for Humanity 4200 Research Forest Dr, Suite 500 The Woodlands, TX 77381 EIN: 88-1104442

name: Turning Point PAC
subject: Our Ground Game Elects America First
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Turning Point PAC, Inc
body:
This is why Democrats are so scared. Ballot chasers and turnout efforts will decide the majority.
---
| | | | | |
---
| | | | | [View in browser](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D "View in Browser")
---
_**A Special Message from our Sponsor**_ _Please note that views expressed in this message do not necessarily reflect those of_ _Parler_ _and do not constitute an endorsement or recommendation by_ _Parler_ _._
---
| Turning Point PAC | | | | | [](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
---
| | Patriot,
---
**The 2026 midterms won’t be decided by TV ads or national polls.**
The midterms will be decided by _whoever has the better ground game_ in must-win state and congressional districts.
Our endorsed candidates are counting on patriots like _you_ because the Democrats are hungry to dismantle the America First movement’s accomplishments brick-by-brick.
But we’re not going to sit back and let them do it without a fight! You can count on that, Patriot.
**The secret ingredient to WINNING the midterms is YOU!**
_Why?_
Well...because American patriots like you help us support the network of staff and volunteers.
That makes the difference between holding the majority or watching Democrats destroy our legacy and set the stage to win 2028.
Your generosity powers the direct efforts that elect these patriots: voter contact tools, chaser training, turnout blitzes... all tied to winning these seats.
**[Continue to help us secure an America First future and help us get our endorsed candidates elected in November>>](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)**

[FIGHT WITH US!](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D)
The future starts with the votes we bank now!
To Victory,
Turning Point PAC
|
---
Non-Contribution Account (For TPPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TPPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TPPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TPPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

Paid for by Turning Point PAC, Inc. (www.tppac.com) and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
| | **_Paid for by Turning Point PAC_**

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Quick update
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26]
Friends, a quick update on our fight to end Trump’s second attempt to illegally tax Americans:
On Friday, we filed a motion with the Court of International Trade to block the implementation of Trump’s latest illegal tariffs.
These taxes are increasing prices even higher during our country’s affordability crisis and inflicting total chaos on our economy. Families and businesses here in California and across the country simply can’t afford to pay the price of this President’s temper tantrum.
If you’re in a position to contribute, will you support our work today with a donation of any amount? Your grassroots support makes all of our work possible. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
Thank you so much for being a part of this fight.
—Rob
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Rob Bonta <info@robbonta.com>
Date: Thurs, Mar 5, 2026
It’s deja vu, folks.
Last year, Trump implemented illegal sweeping global tariffs. We sued. We won.
Now, Trump has implemented illegal sweeping global tariffs. (Again.)
I’m suing. (Again.)
And with the law on our side, we will win. (Again.)
Today I’ve filed my 60th lawsuit against the Trump administration — because when the President breaks the law, we take him to court.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
We’re not going to back down, but we can’t do this alone. I need your support to keep holding Trump and his lawless administration accountable. Will you contribute any amount today to power our work? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
Americans — consumers and business owners — have made it clear they do not want tariffs. These unnecessary taxes are sending costs skyrocketing in an already unaffordable economy! Yet President Trump has tried over and over again to implement them.
This time, he’s attempting to use an obscure law to get his way — but as usual with this President, he’s going about it illegally.
Trump ran on the promise of making life more affordable for families. Instead, he’s breaking the law to raise the cost of living.
It’s not surprising, but it is deeply exasperating.
California has challenged Trump’s illegal tariffs time and time again and on all fronts because these questionable economic decisions have a massive impact on the millions of Californians who are already struggling with rising costs.
And we aren’t going to stop.
Please be a part of this important work holding Trump accountable and protecting Californians and Americans from his reckless and lawless actions — join my team with a donation of any amount today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?express_lane=true&amount=25] DONATE $37 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?express_lane=true&amount=37]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?express_lane=true&amount=50] DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?express_lane=true&amount=250] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26]
Thank you for being in the fight.
In solidarity,
Rob
Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-26?amount=50]
To contribute via check, please address to:
Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026
c/o Deane & Company
1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . Thank you for supporting Rob Bonta. Grassroots supporters like you help us protect California’s working families, advance criminal justice reform, and keep our communities safe. Emails are a crucial way to stay in touch with our top supporters. If we're going to protect and serve the people of California, we need you with us every step of the way.
If you'd still like to unsubscribe from our emails, click unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/tsXL_NR2qEmgRRek5GtgsA2?unsubscribedata=RDuWduFrebFj0i14LLWtYpxZ4UW9C1IEDJ2RLtwE6LLAZ7HqvqJSpKrwJeSIX%2FcpNQjyz6GwodjpHn5HGSXK6wxSsmc5YOJk4ED80jej5nLU0wrB%2FRgtVM8BhnWx%2FdWRRkxyR6wV655gT%2BOnpO9yYVIWJLCUV5RYw14dbbyrT0Lpf%2F27Q1CstUBniRCraUDrVnLWNDbOpOP4GeNY%2FSd210ArxdLKZZypLD%2BIbNB7r35%2BLqjm%2B6el8R%2F92D3VB8IL7ewIIRWt74remsNkG9lADUG%2BadjiTY7TMe6JbCCCCMfOyee0oxJZQnuwCAwwuu8Ttj6KwVkhNk%2FgAly2isyFnEkZrfmIRgQEDc%2B49vTgFuCKjy0k5FOoLw95mhVh%2BVvdS1RcES74IXtAHGnLvNpfvMV1HG2ftOqSCJtSaqNG1S7WazLJtoskSP34aQAG8slZ .
From all of us at Team Bonta, thank you for your support!
PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642

name: Magaziner HQ
subject: firing one person isn’t a long term solution
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress
body:
Seth is in Congress to fight for the middle class and those working hard to join it. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.sethmagaziner.com/p/9_n7o1XMskam_GoKXFDAdg2?unsubscribedata=MIcu7X9gKYXSuvdndFmHBgeZXxiS6dW%2FBUAHCWwzD6c%2FtWQZz3RxgmMq5Ifpzds7mcipFOJMiQBSz%2FIvw3yAqa9E5jj8%2BcolJuLByPBbPro8b%2FQsYFUTpL4PZzUsfs5JypIRpzcMmqHJ9aZNbAMSJk5OaEqoHyqOK4FQZafYaEHVw32lC1HZ70GtscOAvFb8L8gO0PcnoUY5tGNpdMAvs0nHK%2B1JtUwuq%2ByQWGTAPLZr9K7fCnYD4JzoJIjYYKqxuV8fAP6SoTGqGn6kOPRpscSKR1zJN1MO1nwDTdU7c6tjpU%2Fkp5Xu8Mxx440F%2F56jPwpGW4Glq4u78M85RRjDQDAsEstTFBD2UGQDx5s0LKugVx6yKSP2MBtaM0N5ZYl%2BH%2BvJT7XN%2FwL6vwu%2B44SFlcleNYCU5P3tacWMxLhbEu5ZQ%2FyDOIWFXpxLzOMg4Szq)
---
| |
---
Peter,

Since the beginning of this Trump Administration, Seth has made it clear he won't duck tough fights or pretend that staying quiet is leadership.

That’s why he called out Kristi Noem’s lies last year, and why now he is pushing for real reform in the Department of Homeland Security.

In an election year, taking strong positions means we need strong grassroots support behind us. name: Team WFP
subject: We want to hear your thoughts on Iran
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Dear Peter, The ongoing crisis in Iran is both shocking and heartbreaking — with more than 1,300 civilians killed by U.S. and Israeli military action so far, including at least 180 children.1 Congress had a chance to rein in Trump on this war by passing a War Powers Resolution last week. Instead, a handful of Democrats voted with Republicans to stand by while the administration escalates this catastrophic and illegal war. **Soon, Congress could vote to hand the administration tens of billions more in funding for the war in Iran.** That’s money that could be going toward things working families urgently need: like paying for Medicaid for all 16 million Americans who have lost access to healthcare due to Republican budget cuts. Or restoring food stamp benefits cut by Republicans to the 41 million people who rely on them for basic nutrition. [**Before Congress considers voting to fund Trump’s illegal war, we want to hear directly from our supporters — both about how you are feeling in this moment, and how your elected leaders are responding. So we’ve put together a short WFP Supporter Survey to learn where our supporters stand.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRKpjgzvVoraRa6X5vX2XliRCa3Hu7Sco54gr4fc-u59YiB04LzBAcO-iSwR1OtUNoRvbUHJDj4n0VlMTinJ-5ES42ghYTNKsJwmXuUsCo2b-wAwZdtIlSdS_6eVk309S0ourdid3Cnzm0DqAAU2K86RCkXszBLU8m5d5txtDOkBlSuTBWSi2ZtXUyN1cEKkUylQiexhsGqJcSl5_d4a6CDDT9k1TmVD_YZxVtpDfhtGrs6Fto4qY-zdD3UGAeTzNXx0RwuAqB67QaDjSbv5kZkS/4oy/qXQvP1R-SGScneJSODPl4A/h0/h001.KNo0ylOFI410Z89PPfL9lnw39G-BTHBA2foRcKMyMBo) **We read every single response, and we share what we learn directly with WFP leadership.** This is how we make sure strategic decisions aren’t made in a vacuum — and that your experiences aren’t ignored. **[Take the WFP Supporter Survey »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRKpjgzvVoraRa6X5vX2XliRCa3Hu7Sco54gr4fc-u59YiB04LzBAcO-iSwR1OtUNoRvbUHJDj4n0VlMTinJ-5ESl7DeYznwaGCg2MwZH8NJa2ItbDAj__0cHyhe05Oja_dvhpDRPsOtnvvJTJnz44Qjs7F_DFpV-mW5GQtePrATZbi_HNnwhGmyLuKvmQseXxIavweqQbfzA3mH4w38F4EhGHfbIWiH04AEC3YJqLGVMIEtd-peA8sdgAE3Q9WYxVEQOvyikmaRx9We4gCc_h9x/4oy/qXQvP1R-SGScneJSODPl4A/h1/h001.KANqtpYXxIofHvdv-gT0u_bDeKvIAPI2FqKrn2kOEIU)** In solidarity and resistance, Team WFP Source: 1\. clean_body: | | | Dear Peter, The ongoing crisis in Iran is both shocking and heartbreaking — with more than 1,300 civilians killed by U.S. and Israeli military action so far, including at least 180 children.1 Congress had a chance to rein in Trump on this war by passing a War Powers Resolution last week. Instead, a handful of Democrats voted with Republicans to stand by while the administration escalates this catastrophic and illegal war. Soon, Congress could vote to hand the administration tens of billions more in funding for the war in Iran. That’s money that could be going toward things working families urgently need: like paying for Medicaid for all 16 million Americans who have lost access to healthcare due to Republican budget cuts. Or restoring food stamp benefits cut by Republicans to the 41 million people who rely on them for basic nutrition. We read every single response, and we share what we learn directly with WFP leadership. This is how we make sure strategic decisions aren’t made in a vacuum — and that your experiences aren’t ignored. In solidarity and resistance, Team WFP Source: 1\. , UNICEF USA, March 5, 2026 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . My _first_ public fundraising deadline of the year is just around the corner. We have to stay on track of every fundraising goal we set between now and November, which will help us take back the Senate majority.

National Republicans will be watching to see if Democrats across the country have the momentum to win the midterms — _or whether we’ll crack under pressure._

They’ll look for any signs of weakness or loss of momentum in our fundraising numbers. We can’t give them an opening to flood battleground states with attack ads and funnel millions into defeating Democratic candidates. From Texas to New Hampshire, we need to show unequivocal strength.

[**_So please, help us put an end to the GOP’s trifecta of power by chipping in anything that you can before my mid-month deadline at midnight — and you won’t hear from me for a few days!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/127384324/607879651/-671935818?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.03.15_Will-Not-Email-You_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZTFmMmI1NC05NTIwLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6ynBELKhn9T6HyFdPVmLj2XbBkn8M688yCk3dSx6PKk=)

clean_body: Team, I have a proposition for you. = | | = My first public fundraising deadline of the year is just around the corner. name: Houston Gaines
subject: Updates from the Gold Dome
body:
Read up-to-date information from your Capitol
| | | | |
---
| The pace at the Capitol continues to pick up as we've now completed one of the most important milestones of the legislative session--Crossover Day. This deadline marks the day when bills must pass out of the House or Senate (chamber of origin) in order to remain eligible for consideration this year. Please read below for the latest updates from under the Gold Dome.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| LEGISLATIVE UPDATES
---
| Last Friday (3/6) marked Crossover Day. We finished up around 1 a.m. that evening as we debated and voted on dozens of bills. Severals days in recent weeks have gone very late as we passed House bills so they can be considered this Session.
Prior to Crossover Day, the House and Senate gave final approval to House Bill 973, the Amended Fiscal Year 2026 budget. This budget includes:
* $850 million in property tax relief
* $1.17 billion in income tax refunds
* $185 million for Hwy. 316 interchange conversions & $1.68 billion for I-75 S improvements
* $5.6 million for a new UGA School of Nursing
* $409 million for the first state mental health hospital since the 60s

In the week leading up to Crossover Day, the House had passed 97 bills and resolutions addressing issues ranging from tax relief to consumer protections and economic growth. House Bill 1001 and House Bill 880 are two measures focused on reducing the state's income tax. HB 1001 would accelerate Georgia's planned tax cut by lowering the state's flat income tax rate from 5.19% to 4.99% beginning in 2026. HB 880 goes further by proposing a reduction of the income tax rate by 0.10% each year until it reaches 3.99%. It also increases the standard deductions and dependent exemptions to reduce taxable income. Both bills now move to the Senate for further consideration.
The House also passed property tax relief. While a bill to eliminate property taxes on primary residences failed on the House floor (it required a Constitutional majority to pass and the Democrats locked down against the bill), we were able to pass legislation to slow down the rapid growth in property taxes we've seen. It caps revenue growth to 3% for non-improved properties. It also doubles the State Homestead Exemption every other year until 2032.
House Bill 1063 is legislation designed to protect Georgia families and small businesses from bearing the cost of the growing energy demand created by data centers. As Georgia continues to attract major technology investments, this bill ensures that the infrastructure needed to power these facilities is funded through agreements with utilities and the companies themselves--not through higher electricity bills for everyday ratepayers. By putting these safeguards in place, HB 1063 helps support economic growth while also protecting consumers across our state. The bill now moves to the Senate for consideration.
We passed HB 947 to improve health outcomes of SNAP recipients by restricting the use of benefits for certain low-nutrient, high-sugar items, such as candy, energy drinks, sugary desserts, artificially sweetened beverages, and some soft drinks. It strengthens program integrity by adding identification requirements to EBT cards and reinforcing eligibility and necessary work requirements, and it expands partnerships with farmers markets and agricultural vendors to increase access to healthy, SNAP-eligible food options.
One other bill of note is HB 1009 - legislation that bans cell phones in high schools. Last year, we passed similar legislation through 8th grade, but this bill expands that requirement into the high schools to ensure our students are focused on learning. It will minimize distractions in classrooms.
The one bill that the Crossover deadline does not apply to is the budget. Earlier this week, the House passed the Fiscal Year 2027 state budget. It includes nearly $61 million for literacy initiatives aimed at improving early reading skills among students. The funding would support placing literacy coaches in elementary schools across the state. The budget also includes new investments in the Department of Corrections and more funding for mental health initiatives across the state. The balanced budget spending plan now moves to the Georgia Senate for consideration before the state's next fiscal year begins July 1. I will provide a full review of the FY27 budget once it is complete.
---
| |
---
| The House passed two bills I authored prior to Crossover Day, as well: HB 1379 and HB 295.
House Bill 1379 increases transparency and accountability regarding foreign funding in Georgia's public institutions. This legislation would require public colleges and universities and K-12 schools to disclose significant gifts, grants or contracts received from foreign sources to ensure greater oversight of international financial influence. We have found examples of significant money flowing to our schools that may be trying to influence curriculum - and I believe the public should know about it. By strengthening reporting requirements, my bill aims to protect Georgia's students from potential foreign interference while promoting transparency and safeguarding the integrity of education across the state.
House Bill 295 strengthens accountability for local governments by providing property owners with a pathway to seek compensation when a municipality or county fails to enforce laws related to homelessness or illegal immigration or maintains conditions that create a public nuisance. The legislation allows property owners to file claims if they experience financial losses--such as reduced property value or increased security costs--resulting from a local government's pattern of non-enforcement of laws related to public safety and public order. By establishing a clear process for compensation, this bill aims to ensure that local governments uphold their responsibility to enforce existing laws, protect property rights, and maintain safe and orderly communities across Georgia.
As we move beyond Crossover Day, the House is currently reviewing legislation passed by the Senate. Each chamber has until April 2nd to complete this work before we adjourn the 2026 legislative session.
---
| |
---
| IN THE NEWS
---
| [Georgia GOP moving to hold sanctuary cities financially liable for illegal damage ](https://a89bm49ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NhP5Jss8FeG8ozW7uaSljEC6-FNKNYwGW9L-dJx3MJHF-bkaR_Kfee20sOkKiuRj1QWINL4PJnGK7OxMGQCj3Fj7zR_y7BrwSZ7w2MjPI0pqqQ2NIEcwHvB5FT0IOTFuVcWhmYV6dR30ECxCiQSWbodlMGiYS2zSDabww6d9j6IAU1JsYhtjwQPMXykcPeiXCOq0z5MFNBaWc4pGQfAcDWKYp4ym8rwU1nGdYY8rBmitq6wEVUa9xLvxOOGE3U_7FhEssGtIc6I2HTUNiiHeaw==&c=uWpa4XbPCflxjqXuARSKrezjDjeBsO-q7xF6dAOxKjDSHpxEflLI-w==&ch=rsan6p35tthoBnU1eBzBFpHUsbuEsRd2f4c8F81JwHnAiwdQO2-GNQ==)\-- FoxNews "I believe this will work and will hold local governments accountable if they're failing to enforce their own laws and putting their own citizens in dangerous situations ... that's what we're trying to avoid with this legislation."
---
| |
---
| EVENTS AND CAPITOL VISITORS
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| Over the last few weeks, I've been honored to have so many groups from the district visit us at the Capitol. One such group was the Athens Roadrunners - a group of about 50 Athens-area women who came to see the Capitol at work. We also recently welcomed the Oconee Student Advisory Council, a group of student leaders from Oconee County high schools, and 4-H leaders from around the state, including Clarke County. If there is ever an event in our community where you'd like for me to attend or speak, please reach out and let us know. I also invite you to join us at the Capitol one day this Session to see your legislature at work.
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| This week, we had three U.S. Cabinet members visit the Capitol: Small Business Administrator Kelly Loeffler, Secretary of Veterans Affairs Doug Collins, and Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins. It was great to see all three, especially Administrator Loeffler and Secretary Collins to thank these Georgians for the important work they are doing for our country.
---
| |
---
| PAGE PROGRAM
---
| Liam Hamilton, Mitchell Cozart, and Ben Brown recently served as pages at the Capitol. The page program gives students the opportunity to spend a day with us in the House, learning firsthand about the work we do under the Gold Dome and how state government operates. We're grateful to these outstanding students for taking the time to visit the Capitol and for their interest in being involved and engaged in the legislative process. If you know of a student who may be interested in paging, please reach out anytime.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| WHAT'S NEXT?
---
| We are officially through Legislative Day 31 of our 40 Day Session, so we are nearing the end. Sine Die is April 2nd. As we approach this important deadline, we're focused on advancing meaningful legislation and continuing the work on behalf of our district.
As always, we stand ready to help with any questions, comments, or concerns that you may have. Please reach out if I can ever be of assistance. Thank you for allowing me the honor to serve as your State Representative!
---

clean_body: Read up-to-date information from your Capitol | | | | | | The pace at the Capitol continues to pick up as we've now completed one of the most important milestones of the legislative session--Crossover Day. This deadline marks the day when bills must pass out of the House or Senate (chamber of origin) in order to remain eligible for consideration this year. Please read below for the latest updates from under the Gold Dome. | | | | | LEGISLATIVE UPDATES | Last Friday (3/6) marked Crossover Day. We finished up around 1 a.m. that evening as we debated and voted on dozens of bills. Severals days in recent weeks have gone very late as we passed House bills so they can be considered this Session. Prior to Crossover Day, the House and Senate gave final approval to House Bill 973, the Amended Fiscal Year 2026 budget. This budget includes: $850 million in property tax relief $1.17 billion in income tax refunds $185 million for Hwy. 316 interchange conversions & $1.68 billion for I-75 S improvements $5.6 million for a new UGA School of Nursing * $409 million for the first state mental health hospital since the 60s In the week leading up to Crossover Day, the House had passed 97 bills and resolutions addressing issues ranging from tax relief to consumer protections and economic growth. House Bill 1001 and House Bill 880 are two measures focused on reducing the state's income tax. HB 1001 would accelerate Georgia's planned tax cut by lowering the state's flat income tax rate from 5.19% to 4.99% beginning in 2026. HB 880 goes further by proposing a reduction of the income tax rate by 0.10% each year until it reaches 3.99%. It also increases the standard deductions and dependent exemptions to reduce taxable income. Both bills now move to the Senate for further consideration. The House also passed property tax relief. While a bill to eliminate property taxes on primary residences failed on the House floor (it required a Constitutional majority to pass and the Democrats locked down against the bill), we were able to pass legislation to slow down the rapid growth in property taxes we've seen. It caps revenue growth to 3% for non-improved properties. It also doubles the State Homestead Exemption every other year until 2032. House Bill 1063 is legislation designed to protect Georgia families and small businesses from bearing the cost of the growing energy demand created by data centers. As Georgia continues to attract major technology investments, this bill ensures that the infrastructure needed to power these facilities is funded through agreements with utilities and the companies themselves--not through higher electricity bills for everyday ratepayers. By putting these safeguards in place, HB 1063 helps support economic growth while also protecting consumers across our state. The bill now moves to the Senate for consideration. We passed HB 947 to improve health outcomes of SNAP recipients by restricting the use of benefits for certain low-nutrient, high-sugar items, such as candy, energy drinks, sugary desserts, artificially sweetened beverages, and some soft drinks. It strengthens program integrity by adding identification requirements to EBT cards and reinforcing eligibility and necessary work requirements, and it expands partnerships with farmers markets and agricultural vendors to increase access to healthy, SNAP-eligible food options. One other bill of note is HB 1009 - legislation that bans cell phones in high schools. Last year, we passed similar legislation through 8th grade, but this bill expands that requirement into the high schools to ensure our students are focused on learning. It will minimize distractions in classrooms. The one bill that the Crossover deadline does not apply to is the budget. Earlier this week, the House passed the Fiscal Year 2027 state budget. It includes nearly $61 million for literacy initiatives aimed at improving early reading skills among students. The funding would support placing literacy coaches in elementary schools across the state. The budget also includes new investments in the Department of Corrections and more funding for mental health initiatives across the state. The balanced budget spending plan now moves to the Georgia Senate for consideration before the state's next fiscal year begins July 1. I will provide a full review of the FY27 budget once it is complete. | | | The House passed two bills I authored prior to Crossover Day, as well: HB 1379 and HB 295. House Bill 1379 increases transparency and accountability regarding foreign funding in Georgia's public institutions. This legislation would require public colleges and universities and K-12 schools to disclose significant gifts, grants or contracts received from foreign sources to ensure greater oversight of international financial influence. We have found examples of significant money flowing to our schools that may be trying to influence curriculum - and I believe the public should know about it. By strengthening reporting requirements, my bill aims to protect Georgia's students from potential foreign interference while promoting transparency and safeguarding the integrity of education across the state. House Bill 295 strengthens accountability for local governments by providing property owners with a pathway to seek compensation when a municipality or county fails to enforce laws related to homelessness or illegal immigration or maintains conditions that create a public nuisance. The legislation allows property owners to file claims if they experience financial losses--such as reduced property value or increased security costs--resulting from a local government's pattern of non-enforcement of laws related to public safety and public order. By establishing a clear process for compensation, this bill aims to ensure that local governments uphold their responsibility to enforce existing laws, protect property rights, and maintain safe and orderly communities across Georgia. As we move beyond Crossover Day, the House is currently reviewing legislation passed by the Senate. Each chamber has until April 2nd to complete this work before we adjourn the 2026 legislative session. | | | IN THE NEWS | ==\-- FoxNews "I believe this will work and will hold local governments accountable if they're failing to enforce their own laws and putting their own citizens in dangerous situations ... that's what we're trying to avoid with this legislation." | | | EVENTS AND CAPITOL VISITORS | | | | | | | Over the last few weeks, I've been honored to have so many groups from the district visit us at the Capitol. One such group was the Athens Roadrunners - a group of about 50 Athens-area women who came to see the Capitol at work. We also recently welcomed the Oconee Student Advisory Council, a group of student leaders from Oconee County high schools, and 4-H leaders from around the state, including Clarke County. If there is ever an event in our community where you'd like for me to attend or speak, please reach out and let us know. I also invite you to join us at the Capitol one day this Session to see your legislature at work. | | | | | | | This week, we had three U.S. Cabinet members visit the Capitol: Small Business Administrator Kelly Loeffler, Secretary of Veterans Affairs Doug Collins, and Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins. It was great to see all three, especially Administrator Loeffler and Secretary Collins to thank these Georgians for the important work they are doing for our country. | | | PAGE PROGRAM | Liam Hamilton, Mitchell Cozart, and Ben Brown recently served as pages at the Capitol. The page program gives students the opportunity to spend a day with us in the House, learning firsthand about the work we do under the Gold Dome and how state government operates. We're grateful to these outstanding students for taking the time to visit the Capitol and for their interest in being involved and engaged in the legislative process. If you know of a student who may be interested in paging, please reach out anytime. | | | | | | | | | WHAT'S NEXT? | We are officially through Legislative Day 31 of our 40 Day Session, so we are nearing the end. Sine Die is April 2nd. As we approach this important deadline, we're focused on advancing meaningful legislation and continuing the work on behalf of our district. As always, we stand ready to help with any questions, comments, or concerns that you may have. Please reach out if I can ever be of assistance. Thank you for allowing me the honor to serve as your State Representative! | == == == | | == | OUR OFFICE 401-A Coverdell Legislative Office Building Atlanta, GA 30334 404.656.5025 Houston.Gaines@house.ga.gov | | | | | | Committee to Elect Houston Gaines | P.O. Box 1203 | Athens, GA 30603 US |

name: Chris Pappas
subject: Update on the stakes of our campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=5591%2E582294%2EOgVnQ9)
It’s Chris Pappas.
I want to make sure you know the stakes of our campaign here in New Hampshire.
**Recently CNN said, “Dems have a very real chance of winning the Senate.”**
But, there’s _no path_ to flipping the Senate without keeping the Granite State blue. That’s why I’m asking this grassroots team to step up today:
**[Can you make a contribution of $25, $50, or even just $5 to keep me in the fight to hold New Hampshire’s Senate seat?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260315_1.cbxvEG&refcode=em20260315-1&t=2&akid=5591%2E582294%2EOgVnQ9)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260315_1.cbxvEG&amount=5&refcode=em20260315-1&t=21&akid=5591%2E582294%2EOgVnQ9)**
New Hampshire is and _always has been_ a purple state — and with the Trump Administration’s reckless policies, Democrats have a narrow shot at winning purple states like ours across the country.
There’s a path to winning the Senate, but it depends on us winning in New Hampshire. **And since I don’t take a single dime from corporate PACs, I’m counting on folks like you chipping in to emails like this one to power our work.**
**[So please, chip in any amount today. We’ll put it directly to work in this battleground Senate campaign.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260315_1.cbxvEG&refcode=em20260315-1&t=22&akid=5591%2E582294%2EOgVnQ9)**
Thank you for everything,
Chris
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20260315-1&t=23&refcode2=5591_582294_OgVnQ9&akid=5591%2E582294%2EOgVnQ9)**

Hey,
We are being outraised and outspent by Trump’s handpicked candidate, Mike Rogers, and the MAGA billionaires backing him. _**I need your help to close the gap.** _
Rogers has built a nearly $10M war chest and a Koch-aligned super PAC already launched $1 million in ads to boost him with Michigan voters — EIGHT MONTHS before the general election.
_This will be one of the most competitive and expensive Senate races in the country._ **And there is no path to Democrats taking back the Senate without winning in Michigan.**
Polls show this race is a toss-up. We can win, but only if we have the resources to compete.
Our first official FEC fundraising deadline of the year is coming up on March 31. Everyone will be looking at how much we raise to see if we have built the kind of grassroots support we need to take on Trump, Rogers, and the MAGA billionaires on the other side.

Peter,

**Let me be crystal clear: this could be our LAST CHANCE to ruin the Democrats ' dreams. If that sounds enticing to you,** [**I’m humbly asking you to chip in anything you can afford before midnight to help.**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=d4b1e9c934c14f10a2d68300f4260da4&_e=VV_uIrMLpRx_k-GpAx86A_m5m29lkf8s0jNOTJ6ZXpWW4IVrMMeeydfG6YlpMxBHVqpBiLwscX9Ik3OWQadnL9x7CsaKpyAdolAE8btDhe5RfkqPUEFXEQ--ez4jlQvMXSEah1ECSTC5-bamm5zIk_2ua1edkWfXvpFu7RTwoktV0wvBM49Tqg2rras7t6BQdSvwWHlNaJ-16YXwNQZY3ournOFgDGXqiSXrPLG4Eep-iXc40js9XkiLhQ8UPqjLH5JR-nIAwmAtFUCz_UKX3r-27lajS7s8vNuhcDjvRPekbN4GgIRd0N77OwQroIAaX9s6EjFY0sicG8QANjdRVqNwfVR-ZKiaYj71bfzqhheeLd4zH1wS8nCYAKvwmYbPEbNzTlkhWzS5hdB3KUlIpiQVxKfHqpKYDeGgzJKfKUrfHy9BwxROkV8OCEjd-4lfEQ_LwY4xNe42DHeIlFyVgPUhFKsmPXKvtcV9jQgun3ZOAk0iifmA06mTwPlZwGwBC9iMTwqlzUmHWe9bYOp_XW6oFdu93QFF0dWngG9V_xBR1wWzJIaYii4uP1C8NKk1)

Which dream am I talking about? The dream of flipping Alabama blue and corrupting the rest of America with their Woke Communist Ideology.

Their golden boy, Doug Jones, entered the Alabama governor's race and is already showing hints at pulling off a miracle against me. Already, the Dems have dumped enough money behind Doug to cut my lead in HALF!

We CANNOT take the Democrats lightly. If we keep kicking the can down the road, soon enough I'll be LOSING in the polls as Doug pulls ahead before election day. That's a reality that I refuse to live with.

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? _

** [**HELP COACH TUBERVILLE**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=d4b1e9c934c14f10a2d68300f4260da4&_e=VV_uIrMLpRx_k-GpAx86A_m5m29lkf8s0jNOTJ6ZXpWW4IVrMMeeydfG6YlpMxBHVqpBiLwscX9Ik3OWQadnL9x7CsaKpyAdolAE8btDhe5RfkqPUEFXEQ--ez4jlQvMXSEah1ECSTC5-bamm5zIk_2ua1edkWfXvpFu7RTwoktV0wvBM49Tqg2rras7t6BQPUPu7am-exqyU1IwUKow2xlGrrOGjAm2mC7wF-4qdo8kqKleW-pZKXbtkA_UbnYcMfWL3Nlr7NyFcVC12hUqfirtPARvJeZV1vm5EJJ3OUUKTqOgRm5MGQxl2qiIikVsWjgtDw4r6rlE2Fvxk6mU91JfuIhZf9PhXhJne5slOP99dj8ieFyWy7ygH-vN6QvFlVMI8rN1o80zbrnjyYkZB-LvqRnfqvdTCffYdShA4WPBuhM4HuQkX6DJFQaYIJ9cxPIOZSrmAolsQ_o68QwS58wEScFffUFShIdnrHwMiEtPWloaV1MroNr232FFHIl1Q6bEqKreHk-snjfzUnGOLFQm67AQ4yWZhoBHvjkkeZOi3sW98AprGbgelj0v0G21)

| | Hey there, Peter, Kathleen here. I’ll be honest with you, sometimes I wonder why some people go into politics at all. Don’t get me wrong, I’ll never talk someone out of public service. But if your priority isn’t _serving the public_ , I don’t know why anyone would bother. I’ve seen a lot of wealthy people propped up by corporate backers run for office, win, and then completely abandon the community they pledged to serve. _That’s not what public service is about, and that’s not who I am at all._ At my core, I’m still just a teacher trying to make a difference. I don’t mind the long nights and early mornings because I know that the work I do has a real impact on the people I serve. _If I don’t show up, I’m not just shirking on the job; I’m making life worse for my fellow Utahns._ That’s the kind of work ethic I’m fighting to bring to Congress, Peter. But to get there, I’m going to need your help. **[We’re halfway through March, and I need your support now more than ever if we’re going to win in November. clean_body: What it means to really show up | | | Hey there, Peter, Kathleen here. I’ll be honest with you, sometimes I wonder why some people go into politics at all. Don’t get me wrong, I’ll never talk someone out of public service. But if your priority isn’t serving the public , I don’t know why anyone would bother. I’ve seen a lot of wealthy people propped up by corporate backers run for office, win, and then completely abandon the community they pledged to serve. That’s not what public service is about, and that’s not who I am at all. At my core, I’m still just a teacher trying to make a difference. I don’t mind the long nights and early mornings because I know that the work I do has a real impact on the people I serve. If I don’t show up, I’m not just shirking on the job; I’m making life worse for my fellow Utahns. That’s the kind of work ethic I’m fighting to bring to Congress, Peter. But to get there, I’m going to need your help. = | | = = | = = | = = With gratitude, Kathleen Riebe | * = As a dedicated educator and State Senator, Kathleen Riebe brings real-world experience to the fight for better schools and stronger communities. She works tirelessly to find common-sense solutions that improve the lives of all Utahns. To learn more about Kathleen, visit: = | ---|--- Emails like this are the most effective way to grow our campaign and reach supporters like you.
